title: Rankett
html:
Rankett

Rankett is Yahir's primary venture as of 2026 — described in Memory as "all my energy and time and future banking right now" — and the latest expression of a thread that runs back almost a decade in his notes. The seed idea (dissolving "mindless boring tasks irl" into software) was already named in 2024 05 13 things: parking attendants, toll booths, gym front desks, museum ticket sellers, retail greeters. The wedge that made it specifically AI search visibility shows up in 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream, where Yahir was already brainstorming chatgpt product ideas weeks after launch and writing "Asking the right question will be the next best skill to have." By Jan 2026 he's executing on a sharply defined business with a written GTM bible.

What it actually is

A done-for-you white-label ai visibility engine sold to marketing/SEO agencies — never to end clients, never as a self-serve SaaS. The GTM bible is unambiguous: $998/seat/month from agency to Rankett, agency resells to their client at $3K–$6K/mo, the agency keeps $2K–$5K of pure margin per seat. The category is ai search optimization — visibility across chatgpt, anthropic claude, gemini, perplexity, grok, deepseek, and google ai overviews. The tagline Yahir wrote for himself in 2026 01 13 rankett com is "We whitelabel AI search optimization." Internally the framing is "prompts are the new keywords."

The architecture and the filter

The product is a ghost white label: zero Rankett branding in UI, page source, meta tags, error messages, contracts, or fulfillment bots. Agency's domain, agency's logo, agency's colors. Clients never know Rankett exists. The moat is pooled data network effect — every new agency benefits from results accumulated across the entire Rankett network, so newer agencies sell with proof from older agencies' data. The filter is deliberately narrow: agencies need 5+ existing clients to qualify, and a 60 day activation rule kicks them off the platform if they haven't onboarded one paying client in the first 60 days. Tire-kickers don't survive contact with the funnel.

What gets standardized vs custom

Standardized always: platform setup, weekly reporting (first 4 weeks then monthly), auto-refresh analysis every 10 days, monthly PDF reports, schema markup for ai optimization, llm txt implementation, directory management across 50+ sites. Never custom: writing the agency's ads/emails/landing pages, end-client support, custom features per agency. "Product, not a dev shop." Three user roles — Rankett internal (admin systems, fulfillment), agency (admin portal, client management, branding), end client (read-only white-labeled dashboard with their score and progress). Partner tiers scale support intensity from 1–4 clients (email-only) to 10+ (priority + roadmap input).

Why it carries so much weight

Rankett is the path to the explicit financial milestones in Memory — $5K/mo by month two and $100K/mo by end-of-year 2026 — and the thing that, if it works, makes the gym shift from 2025 09 23 how im scraping by go away. The lived stakes are real: that note documents Yahir selling a gun at 50% off to make rent and asking his dad embarrassingly for an advance. The mid-Jan 2026 2026 01 13 rankett com note also captures him grappling with a tweet that "fucked me up" — someone publicly staking the AI-visibility claim he's been building privately — and deep-thinking whether the space still has merit. The conviction held; the prep guide is dated six days earlier.

Open contradictions

The pooled data network effect is asserted in the GTM bible but it's not yet clear whether data is actually shared cross-agency in product or only in the sales pitch. The 60 day activation rule hasn't been validated at scale. And Rankett's relationship to the (and to brotein, the predecessor primary venture from mid-2025) is not yet mapped — these may be sequential bets, parallel hedges, or one feeding the other.
